title: Which countries include rotavirus vaccines in their national vaccination programs?
subtitle: This shows which countries provide and recommend rotavirus vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.
caption: Across the globe in 2024, national vaccination programs show a wide range of rotavirus vaccine inclusion. The map displays countries where the vaccine is administered to the entire country, not routinely administered, given only in specific regions, or targeted at specific risk groups. The most common status is administration across the entire country, visible in the Americas, most of Europe, Australia, and large parts of Africa. In contrast, countries where the vaccine is not routinely administered are concentrated in parts of North Africa, the Middle East, and Asia, including Russia, China, and India. A smaller number of countries, such as Argentina and Chile, show regional administration, while Portugal is the only country shown to target specific risk groups. Greenland and Western Sahara are marked as having no data. The geographic pattern shows broad coverage in the Western Hemisphere and Oceania, with more limited or non-routine use across much of Asia and North Africa.
